---
title: "Further Reading"
---

## Bayesian inference

- Gelman, A. et al. (2013). *Bayesian Data Analysis* (3rd ed.). Chapman & Hall/CRC.
  The standard reference for Bayesian methods.
- McElreath, R. (2020). *Statistical Rethinking* (2nd ed.). CRC Press.
  An accessible introduction to Bayesian modelling with a focus on building intuition.

## Infectious disease modelling

- Keeling, M.J. & Rohani, P. (2008). *Modeling Infectious Diseases in Humans and Animals*. Princeton University Press.
  Comprehensive treatment of compartmental models and their analysis.
- Bjørnstad, O.N. (2018). *Epidemics: Models and Data Using R*. Springer.
  Practical introduction to fitting epidemic models to data.

## Julia

- [Julia manual](https://docs.julialang.org/en/v1/) — official language documentation
- [Think Julia](https://benlauwens.github.io/ThinkJulia.jl/latest/book.html) — introduction to programming using Julia

## Probabilistic programming

- [Turing.jl tutorials](https://turinglang.org/stable/tutorials/) — worked examples from simple to advanced
- van de Meent, J.-W. et al. (2018). [An Introduction to Probabilistic Programming](https://arxiv.org/abs/1809.10756). arXiv:1809.10756.

## MCMC and computational methods

- Brooks, S. et al. (2011). *Handbook of Markov Chain Monte Carlo*. Chapman & Hall/CRC.
- Betancourt, M. (2017). [A Conceptual Introduction to Hamiltonian Monte Carlo](https://arxiv.org/abs/1701.02434). arXiv:1701.02434.
  Excellent explanation of the NUTS sampler used throughout this course.
